summary: This brief post previews O'Neil's Bloomberg column arguing that a Biden victory would not mark an end to problems but rather the beginning of enormous catch-up work. She emphasizes that the challenges facing America extend far beyond reversing Trump-era damage, particularly regarding the unchecked power of big tech companies. The post frames the post-Trump period as a 'hangover' requiring sustained policy effort rather than celebration.
key_points: A Trump departure would be a 'best case scenario' but not a solution in itself
Big tech's power and malevolence requires urgent attention regardless of who is president
The policy backlog extends beyond simply undoing Trump administration actions
Recovery will require 'massive' work, suggesting the scope of neglected issues
money_quotes: In the best case scenario that Trump is gone in January, we have a massive amount of work to catch up on
There's so much to fix beyond what Trump has broken
key_insight: Political change alone won't solve systemic problems like big tech's unchecked power—the real work of reform begins after the election.
